title: Funding Independent Economic Research and Maintenance of Critical Data Resources
brief: This proposal requests 44,800,000 ATLAS over the period of 360 days in funding from the Star Atlas Ecosystem Fund to provide investors and players with transparent economic and financial information surrounding the Star Atlas Ecosystem. This amount will be distributed in four 11,200,000 ATLAS increments over four sub-PIPs, each with a span of 90 days. The funding is meant to ensure that the Star ATLAS community continues to have economic data resources, even in the face of structural breaks in data resources and in the face of uncertainty from rising costs.
description:
# PIP 19: Funding Independent Economic Research and Maintenance of Critical Data Resources [Comprehensive PIP]

> ### Comprehensive PIP-19 phase 1: This vote will only approve funds for PIP-19A 

# 1. Header

**Title:** Funding Independent Economic Research and Maintenance of Critical Data Resources
**Author:** Steven Sabol
**Category:** Funding

---

# 2. Summary

This proposal requests 44,800,000 ATLAS over the period of 360 days in funding from the Star Atlas Ecosystem Fund to provide investors and players with transparent economic and financial information surrounding the Star Atlas Ecosystem. This amount will be distributed in four 11,200,000 ATLAS increments over four sub-PIPs, each with a span of 90 days. The funding is meant to ensure that the Star ATLAS community continues to have economic data resources, even in the face of structural breaks in data resources and in the face of uncertainty from rising costs.  
* This PIP requires funding to migrate data resources over to Snowflake from Flipside Studio. Roughly 44% of the amount is to be applied towards the recent increased computational cost of querying blockchain data, and will go towards a budget for Snowflake Credits and AWS cloud services.
* Ongoing research into metaverse economics, building on prior work that has enhanced transparency around risks and opportunities in metaverse gaming.
* Improved access to on-chain Star Atlas data for researchers on popular platforms.

**The first 11,200,000 ATLAS payment is for this sub-PIP-19A. For the full PIP-19 implementation details and cost breakdown, see: [PIP 19 Timeline and Details](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1jx56XBf2lM_yMsM-9wVDXOyBFqhqnucRFAKPvNWkCe8/edit?usp=sharing)**

---

# 3. Motivation

* This proposal seeks 11,200,000 ATLAS for Star Atlas Economic Reports and Statistical Releases (See the link).  The Star Atlas metaverse is an increasingly complex economic system requiring robust data to support investment decisions, measure returns, and evaluate tokenomics. 
* This proposal ensures:
  * Long-term maintenance of critical data resources for Star Atlas investors and Decentralized Autonomous Corporations (DACs).
  * Continued independent, open-source economic research led by an experienced former Federal Reserve researcher and Star Atlas economics team member.

___

# 4. Specification

The funding will enhance transparency and data dissemination in three key areas: 

### 4.1 Ecosystem Financial Data

* *Star ATLAS Monthly Tokenomics Update:* Independent written assessment of essential trends in ATLAS and POLIS tokenomics. Deliverable: X Threads and an Accessible Google Sheet With Underlying data. Frequency: Monthly, First Friday After The End of the Previous Month.
* *DAO Dashboard:* A custom dataset for the DAO.  Deliverable: Google sheet with custom charts. Frequency: Updated bi-weekly.
* *DAC Starbased CORE Earnings Report:* Track DAC profits, membership, expenses, and external revenues. Deliverable: Google Sheet. Frequency: Quarter Ends.
* *Bespoke Analysis:* Respond to community data requests to support bespoke analyses. Deliverable: Google Sheet or Custom Report, Structured as a PDF. Frequency: Irregular.
* *2025 Year-End Tax Tool (Possibly Part of PIP-20B):* Wallet Look-up for earnings and expenses incurred throughout the entire ecosystem. Jan/1/2025 - December 31/2025. Deliverable: Google Sheet. Frequency: Annual.

### 4.2 Metaverse Economic Research and Risk Analysis

* *Long-Term Research Project:* Measure player external wealth and add to the total value of Star Atlas assets for total wealth. Deliverable: X Thread and written report, along with an accessible Google Sheet containing the data. Frequency: Quarterly.

### 4.3 Enhanced Investor Research Experience

* Improve data accessibility for traditional and blockchain analysts.
* *Wallet and Program Labelling and Tagging on Public Websites:* Correctly classify and tag over 85 known Star Atlas wallets and programs on Dune and Solscan. Frequency: This is a one-time process for current wallets but requires active maintenance for new wallets and programs.

> **Note:** Please see the [“PIP20 Implementation Details and Cost Breakdown”](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1jx56XBf2lM_yMsM-9wVDXOyBFqhqnucRFAKPvNWkCe8/edit?usp=sharing) for more information.

___

# 5. Stakeholder Impact

* *Players:* Gain access to comprehensive earnings and expense data, enabling better economic strategies and transparency under evolving conditions.
* *DACs:* Benefit from standardized reporting dashboards, ensuring equitable access to economic performance data regardless of analytical capacity.
* *DAO:* Custom tools that help make the council more effective, more informed, and provide them with a superior understanding of the economy.
* *External analysts:* Gain better tools for studying Star Atlas’s economic impact on Solana and vice versa.

Players with limited data literacy may find dashboards overwhelming; however, this can be mitigated by providing user guides.

___

# 6. Implementation Details

* Snowflake will be used to construct economic datasets and reports, which will be distributed using Google Sheets. 
* *See the following for example datasets:* [Datasets](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1xzfobyaTJJKLF1bKYH5dRyQaYiQ9GMW-TR2dLW3kvww/edit?usp=sharing)
* Reports on community wealth, income, emissions, and expenses will be shared publicly via regular updates on X and Discord (e.g., quarterly reports and ad-hoc announcements). The frequency of data updates is meant to balance the cost of querying the data with the relevance of the information.

___

# 7. Timeline

### 7.1 PIP-19A

* *Wallet and Program Labelling and Tagging on Public Websites:* Within 45 days of initial funding for 20A.
* *Star ATLAS Monthly Tokenomics Update:* **Within 30 days** of initial funding for 20A, and on a recurring basis.
* *Long-Term Research Project:* **Within 90 days** of initial funding for 20A.
* *DAO Dashboard:* **Within 90 days** of initial funding.
* *DAC Starbased CORE Earnings Report:* **Within 60 days** of initial funding for 20A.
* *2025 Year End Tax Tool (Possibly Part of PIP-20B):* **Jan 15, 2026**.

### 7.2 Dependencies:

* Timely approval of funding from the Star Atlas Ecosystem Fund.
* Continued availability of on-chain data from Flipside in Snowflake.

___

# 8. Cost & Resource Allocation (PIP-19A)

### 8.1 Total Recurring Cost:  

* 11,200,000 ATLAS for 90 days.  
* 5,000,000 ATLAS for estimated Snowflake and AWS costs to update and build datasets, and 
* 6,200,000 ATLAS for the labor, which includes dataset maintenance and research expertise. 
  * A detailed breakdown of how these costs are distributed is found below for each of the major deliverables.

### 8.2 Detailed Breakdown

* *Wallet and Program Labelling and Tagging on Public Websites:* Labor costs to find addresses and submit labels and tags.
* *Star ATLAS Monthly Tokenomics Update:* Labor cost required to write and craft a narrative each month. Snowflake query costs to update the data.
* *Long-Term Research Project:* Snowflake query credits and research time.
* *DAO Dashboard:* Snowflake credits for updates once every two weeks, and the initial build. Labor costs.
* *DAC Starbased CORE Earnings Report:* Snowflake query credits each quarter to update. Labor costs required to incorporate new game loops.
* *2025 Year-End Tax Tool (Possibly Part of PIP-19B):* Snowflake credits and research into updated economic game-loops embedded in C4.

### 8.3 Funding Source: 

* Star Atlas Ecosystem Fund

### 8.4 Management personnel: 

* Steven Sabol

___

# 9. Additional Details

* **Conflicts of Interest:** I am the former Director of Economy for ATMTA.
* **Related Proposals:** Aligns with PIP-4 (Ecosystem Fund) and PIP-1 (Sustainable Governance).
* **Risks:** 
  * Potential challenges include data platform outages or changes in on-chain data structures, which will be mitigated through proactive monitoring and contingency planning.
  * Players with limited data literacy may find dashboards overwhelming; however, this can be mitigated by providing user guides.
